# Audio test fixtures

Real-speech WAV files used by the LFM2.5-Audio ASR integration
tests in `wick/tests/session_chain.rs`. Each file is 16 kHz mono
signed 16-bit PCM (the canonical input format `Session::append_audio`
expects) wrapped in a RIFF/WAVE container.

## Files

| File | Phrase | Duration | Audio frames (encoder out) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `today_is_a_beautiful_day.wav` | "Today is a beautiful day" | ~1.6 s | 22 |

The frame count matches `llama.cpp`'s `llama-mtmd-cli` output
(`n_tokens_batch = 22`) on the same WAV with the same mmproj —
verified locally as a sanity check on the wick audio encoder
(mel preprocessor + Conformer conv stem + adapter all produce
the right shape).

## Test contract

`asr_real_audio_matches_input_phrase` runs the WAV through the
full chat-template + audio-input flow (system="Perform ASR.")
with `max_tokens=24` greedy decode at temperature 0, and asserts
the transcription matches `"Today is a beautiful day."` exactly
(case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed).

The expected output is byte-identical to what `llama.cpp`'s
`llama-mtmd-cli` produces on the same audio + same Q4_0 weights
+ same chat template — both implementations are deterministic at
temperature 0.

## Regenerating

The committed `.wav` files were produced by macOS `say` +
`afconvert`. Run `./generate.sh` from this directory (macOS only)
to reproduce. The script pins `say -v Samantha` (a built-in
en-US voice on every stock macOS install since Sierra) so
regeneration is reproducible across hosts — without an explicit
voice the default varies per user / OS version / language
settings.

## Why external TTS instead of wick's own

The fixtures are inputs to wick's ASR path. If they were
generated by wick's own TTS, a regression in the LLM would shift
both directions of a self-loop in lockstep — the test would
still pass on garbage. macOS `say` is a fixed external
reference: a wick regression in either direction shows up as a
transcription that no longer matches the input phrase.
